label: Section 1
title: Transparency and modalities
summary: Sets the common conditions for clear communications and for facilitating the exercise of data-subject rights, including response timing, identity checks, fees, refusals, and standardized notices.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 2
title: Information and access to personal data
summary: Organizes the information controllers must provide when data are obtained directly or indirectly and the data subject's right to confirmation, access, and a copy of personal data.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 3
title: Rectification and erasure
summary: Groups rights to correct, erase, restrict, and port personal data with related recipient-notification duties, each subject to the conditions and limits stated in the individual articles.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 4
title: Right to object and automated individual decision-making
summary: Covers objections to specified processing and safeguards for certain decisions based solely on automated processing, including profiling, while preserving the articles' distinct triggers and exceptions.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 5
title: Restrictions
summary: Defines the conditions and safeguards under which Union or Member State law may restrict specified GDPR rights and obligations for listed public-interest objectives.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 1
title: General obligations
summary: Builds the core controller-and-processor accountability architecture through responsibility, data protection by design and default, role allocation, processor governance, records, and regulatory cooperation.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 2
title: Security of personal data
summary: Links risk-appropriate security of processing with duties to document and notify qualifying personal-data breaches to supervisory authorities and, in specified cases, affected individuals.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 3
title: Data protection impact assessment and prior consultation
summary: Structures prospective assessment of processing likely to create high risks and consultation with the supervisory authority when identified residual risks are not sufficiently mitigated.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 4
title: Data protection officer
summary: Defines when a data protection officer is required and frames the officer's position, independence, resources, access, advisory work, monitoring, and supervisory-authority contact role.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 5
title: Codes of conduct and certification
summary: Creates voluntary accountability mechanisms through approved codes of conduct and data-protection certification, together with rules for monitoring and certification bodies.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 1
title: Independent status
summary: Establishes independent supervisory authorities and frames their institutional independence, member qualifications, appointment conditions, resources, and domestic legal foundations.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 2
title: Competence, tasks and powers
summary: Allocates supervisory competence, including lead-authority competence, and sets out authorities' public tasks, investigative and corrective powers, authorization roles, and reporting duties.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 1
title: Cooperation
summary: Coordinates cross-border supervision through lead-authority cooperation, mutual assistance, and joint operations among supervisory authorities, with procedures and time-sensitive response duties.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 2
title: Consistency
summary: Provides Union-level consistency procedures for Board opinions, binding dispute resolution, urgent action, and information exchange when supervisory decisions may have wider effects.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: Section 3
title: European data protection board
summary: Defines the European Data Protection Board's composition, independence, tasks, procedures, leadership, secretariat support, reporting, and confidentiality arrangements.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, definitions, exceptions, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 1
title: Classification of AI systems as high-risk
summary: Sets the routes for classifying AI systems as high-risk and the mechanism for updating Annex III use cases, including criteria relevant to additions and removals.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 2
title: Requirements for high-risk AI systems
summary: Defines the main lifecycle requirements for high-risk AI systems: risk management, data governance, documentation, logs, deployer information, human oversight, accuracy, robustness, and cybersecurity.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 3
title: Obligations of providers and deployers of high-risk AI systems and other parties
summary: Allocates operational and documentation duties across providers, representatives, importers, distributors, deployers, and other value-chain actors, including corrective action and specified impact-assessment duties.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 4
title: Notifying authorities and notified bodies
summary: Creates the notification and oversight architecture for conformity-assessment bodies, covering authority duties, body competence and independence, notification procedures, subcontracting, coordination, and third-country arrangements.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 5
title: Standards, conformity assessment, certificates, registration
summary: Connects standards and common specifications to conformity presumptions and assessment routes, then addresses certificates, derogations, declarations, CE marking, and registration.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 1
title: Classification rules
summary: Defines how a general-purpose AI model may be classified as presenting systemic risk through capability thresholds or Commission designation and provides the related procedural route.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 2
title: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models
summary: Sets baseline provider duties for general-purpose AI models concerning technical documentation, information for downstream providers, copyright policy, training-content summaries, and representation for certain non-Union providers.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 3
title: Obligations of providers of general-purpose AI models with systemic risk
summary: Adds systemic-risk duties for model evaluation, adversarial testing, assessment and mitigation, serious-incident reporting, and cybersecurity protection for models meeting the section's classification conditions.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 4
title: Codes of practice
summary: Provides a process for developing, assessing, and approving codes of practice that support application of general-purpose AI model obligations without replacing the underlying legal requirements.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 1
title: Governance at Union level
summary: Establishes the Union-level governance network around the AI Office, AI Board, advisory forum, scientific panel, and expert pool, with distinct coordination and advisory functions.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 2
title: National competent authorities
summary: Requires Member States to designate national competent authorities and single points of contact and addresses their organization, resources, independence, coordination, and reporting.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 1
title: Post-market monitoring
summary: Requires providers of high-risk AI systems to establish proportionate post-market monitoring and a documented plan for collecting and analyzing performance data throughout the system's lifetime.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 2
title: Sharing of information on serious incidents
summary: Frames provider reporting and follow-up for serious incidents involving high-risk AI systems, including timing, investigation, cooperation, and coordination with relevant authorities.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 3
title: Enforcement
summary: Sets the market-surveillance and enforcement framework for AI systems, including authority powers, mutual assistance, fundamental-rights oversight, risk procedures, safeguards, confidentiality, and Union support.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 4
title: Remedies
summary: Provides channels for complaints to market-surveillance authorities, explanations for certain individual decisions based on high-risk AI outputs, and protected reporting of infringements.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: SECTION 5
title: Supervision, investigation, enforcement and monitoring in respect of providers of general-purpose AI models
summary: Defines the AI Office's monitoring and enforcement toolkit for general-purpose AI model providers, including information requests, evaluations, corrective measures, scientific-panel alerts, and procedural rights.

editorial.note: Orientation only; consult the complete section text, annexes, exceptions, application dates, and current interpretation.

label: MAP function
title: Establish context and identify risks
summary: The MAP function organizes voluntary outcomes for understanding an AI system's context, purposes, affected people, dependencies, knowledge limits, benefits, and potential harms before risks are measured and managed.

sourceBasis.label: NIST — Artificial Intelligence Risk Management Framework (AI RMF 1.0)

editorial.note: Voluntary-framework orientation only; this summary does not convert outcomes into legal duties or reproduce the complete framework.

label: International Guiding Principles
title: Guiding principles for advanced AI systems and AI actors
summary: The Guiding Principles present a non-binding lifecycle agenda for advanced AI risk identification, testing, reporting, incident information, security, transparency, content authentication, standards, and responsible data practices.

sourceBasis.label: Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan — Hiroshima AI Process documents

editorial.note: Soft-law orientation only; this summary does not imply legal force, equivalence to law, or completeness.

label: Values-based Principles 1.1–1.5
title: Responsible stewardship of trustworthy AI
summary: The values-based principles organize responsible stewardship around inclusive benefit, human rights and democratic values, transparency, lifecycle robustness and safety, and accountability by AI actors.

sourceBasis.label: OECD/LEGAL/0449 — Recommendation of the Council on Artificial Intelligence

editorial.note: Intergovernmental-principles orientation only; this summary does not imply legal equivalence or reproduce the complete recommendation.
